title: QUIC RFC 9002 Retry Recovery Reset Verification
status_summary: This verification artifact records the landed Retry recovery reset slice and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence in the working tree. The targeted Retry requirement-home tests and Retry integrity unit tests passed; RTT-estimation follow-ons remain out of scope.
scope:
This verification covers the RFC 9002 Section 6.3 Retry slice: the client treats Retry as a connection-establishment reroute, resets congestion and loss-recovery state, and preserves the TLS handshake transcript needed to replay the bootstrap ClientHello.
It does not claim RTT-estimation policy beyond Retry receipt or broader Retry handling outside the three Section 6.3 requirements.
expected_result:
- Retry is not treated as ACK.
- Retry resets client congestion and loss-recovery state, including pending PTO state.
- Retry preserves the bootstrap handshake transcript so the replay path can resend the same ClientHello bytes.
preconditions: The RFC9002 Retry requirement homes are discoverable by the local test inventory.
The Retry integrity unit tests and targeted requirement-home tests are runnable in the local workbench flow.
procedure: Run the targeted dotnet test slice for the RFC 9002 Retry family and the Retry integrity unit tests.
Inspect the runtime Retry path and the requirement-home assertions for recovery reset and handshake transcript retention.
Render the trace markdown siblings from the canonical JSON artifacts so the downstream reports stay in sync.
